name: Keelung (Taiwan)

colors.symbolism: the harbour, the Pacific, and Keelung's identity as a port city
the warm sun over the harbour and prosperity

description: A blue field charged at the centre with a white-and-yellow emblem stylising the city's name (基隆) in flowing forms suggestive of waves and a harbour.
history: Keelung is a provincial city on Taiwan's northern coast, occupying a deep natural harbour and serving as the principal northern port of the country. The current city flag has been in use since the late 20th century. The emblem stylises the characters 基隆 ('foundation prosperity') alongside maritime and harbour motifs.

features.symbolism: Keelung's natural harbour and the Pacific Ocean
stylised form of the characters 基隆 with flowing maritime motifs, expressing the city's identity as Taiwan's principal northern port
